caveats: a: BLS covers the broader occupation and other-personal-services group, not standalone 812922 firms.
a: CVS is an embedded national retailer rather than an LMM standalone lab, but it exposes the current self-service workflow.
a: The frozen labor-share input uses 2024 wages over 2022 receipts and was not re-estimated.
rho: A digital ordering interface is evidence of baseline automation, not incremental AI savings.
rho: Large-retailer technology may not be affordable or portable to independent labs.
e: The estimate is a structural judgment, not a measured census of independent operators.
e: The frozen n value is an ESTIMATE of zero based on a cross-industry margin; it may miss unusual LMM firms but was not replaced.
s5: The absence of a defensible denominator is treated as missing, not as a zero transfer probability.
s5: No public transaction series isolates standalone one-hour photofinishing firms.
q: The cited prices and promotions are from a large retailer, not standalone LMM labs.
q: No source reports the share of AI-enabled gross benefit retained by operators.
d5: The revenue series is nominal, ends in 2022, and combines both photofinishing industries.
d5: The subindustry haircut is judgmental because no recent standalone one-hour quantity series was found.
d5: Retail same-day products may persist even as separately classified one-hour establishments disappear.
o: CVS demonstrates an online-to-store workflow but not the share requiring a standalone photofinishing operator.
o: Demand eliminated by digital-only behavior belongs in d5; o addresses only the surviving year-five quantity.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Customers pay transparent per-print or per-product prices for rapid physical output, often ordering through a website, app, smartphone, or kiosk before same-day pickup. The model is transaction based, with convenience and local turnaround providing the principal service premium.
lens.excluded_activities: Non-one-hour laboratories classified in 812921; motion-picture film processing; photo departments embedded in retailers when not separate photofinishing establishments; self-service equipment vendors; software-only image storage or editing; captive internal units; and firms outside the approximate $1-10M normalized EBITDA band.
lens.included_activities: Lower-middle-market establishments known as one-hour photofinishing labs that serve external customers by developing still film or making photographic slides, prints, and enlargements on a short-turnaround or while-you-wait basis, including digitally ordered same-day pickup when the establishment's primary activity is photofinishing.

caveats: BLS covers the broader occupation and other-personal-services group, not standalone 812922 firms.
CVS is an embedded national retailer rather than an LMM standalone lab, but it exposes the current self-service workflow.
The frozen labor-share input uses 2024 wages over 2022 receipts and was not re-estimated.
a.change_evidence: Time-and-motion and payroll-by-role data from standalone one-hour labs covering order assistance, image review, production monitoring, quality control, reprints, maintenance, and pickup would materially refine a.
a.rationale: Online and kiosk ordering already automate selection, layout, payment, and file transfer. Remaining exposed tasks include customer support, exception handling, basic image-quality review, production monitoring, order communication, and some administrative work; loading supplies, maintaining equipment, resolving print defects, and physical handoff remain. The range is a wage-weighted task judgment rather than an observed measure.

d5.bridge: Census Service Annual Survey data for the combined 81292 group show nominal revenue declining from $1.255 billion in 2018 to $991 million in 2022, while BLS explicitly attributes lower photoprocess-worker use to self-printing and fewer physical prints. The combined nominal series likely overstates constant-price one-hour quantity because non-one-hour professional, mail-order, and specialty products are more diversified; an additional haircut produces a 0.55 five-year base for 812922.
caveats: The revenue series is nominal, ends in 2022, and combines both photofinishing industries.
The subindustry haircut is judgmental because no recent standalone one-hour quantity series was found.
Retail same-day products may persist even as separately classified one-hour establishments disappear.
d5.change_evidence: Annual 812922-only constant-price receipts, print units, same-day orders, and standalone location counts, separated from retailer departments and non-one-hour fulfillment, would replace the proxy.
d5.rationale: Same-day physical prints and gifts retain convenience value, but smartphones, cloud storage, online-only sharing, home printers, and retailer self-service have already replaced much routine one-hour demand. The wide range allows for residual gift and urgent-print demand while recognizing continued structural contraction.

caveats: The estimate is a structural judgment, not a measured census of independent operators.
The frozen n value is an ESTIMATE of zero based on a cross-industry margin; it may miss unusual LMM firms but was not replaced.
e.change_evidence: A Business Register and firm-level audit of 812922 establishments linking ownership, primary activity, external photo revenue, standalone control, normalized EBITDA, and retailer affiliation would determine whether any eligible firms exist.
e.rationale: The service is external and repeat, but most surviving same-day photo capacity appears embedded in large pharmacy or mass-retail networks rather than independently controlled LMM photofinishing firms. The frozen firm-count chain returns zero in the target EBITDA band, and the small high bound allows for a rare standalone regional operator without treating retailer departments as transferable companies.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 812922 ($15,011,255) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 812922 ($33,260,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[81]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $115,486 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 812922); class <5: 79 firms, avg revenue/firm $206,367 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $32,296 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 12 firms, avg revenue/firm $601,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $94,056 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 3 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,261,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $197,346 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 0

caveats: CVS demonstrates an online-to-store workflow but not the share requiring a standalone photofinishing operator.
Demand eliminated by digital-only behavior belongs in d5; o addresses only the surviving year-five quantity.
o.change_evidence: Order-level data separating staff-assisted production, unattended kiosks, home printing, shipped central-lab fulfillment, software-only editing, cancellations, and reprints would materially move o.
o.rationale: Customers can select and transmit images without staff, but surviving demand for rapid physical prints still requires maintained equipment, consumables, output quality, and local fulfillment accountability. Some quantity can be fully self-served through kiosks or home printers, so the operator-required share is lower than for specialty labs.

caveats: The cited prices and promotions are from a large retailer, not standalone LMM labs.
No source reports the share of AI-enabled gross benefit retained by operators.
q.change_evidence: Store- and order-level evidence on realized prices, promotions, price matching, reprints, support cost, technology spend, and contribution margin before and after automation would materially refine q.
q.rationale: Operators can initially retain labor savings under posted per-print prices, but same-day products have transparent online prices, frequent percentage-off promotions, low customer switching costs, and competition from home printing and large retailers. Software, integration, reprints, and store support absorb additional benefit.

caveats: A digital ordering interface is evidence of baseline automation, not incremental AI savings.
Large-retailer technology may not be affordable or portable to independent labs.
rho.change_evidence: Deployment data from same-day labs showing automatic-order share, support deflection, exception rates, reprints, customer complaints, labor hours, and equipment downtime before and after AI adoption would materially move rho.
rho.rationale: Digital file intake and standardized products make support, image checks, exception routing, and customer messaging technically automatable. The remaining independent population is very small, integration budgets are limited, and rapid-turnaround quality failures require local intervention, constraining five-year operational realization.

caveats: The absence of a defensible denominator is treated as missing, not as a zero transfer probability.
No public transaction series isolates standalone one-hour photofinishing firms.
s5.change_evidence: A verified list of standalone eligible 812922 firms and five years of completed control transactions from broker, lender, and business-registry records, excluding retailer and equipment-only events, would be required to estimate s5.
s5.rationale: No defensible five-year control-transfer probability was found for a standalone LMM one-hour-lab population, and the frozen dataset identifies no firms in the band. Retail-chain ownership changes, store closures, kiosk replacements, and sales of embedded photo departments are not qualifying transfers of an eligible operating firm.

principal_driver: Rapid local physical fulfillment still requires equipment and quality accountability after the digital order is placed.
principal_weakness: The estimated LMM population is zero and the remaining workflow is both structurally shrinking and substantially self-served already.
